message:
chore(beads): sync close_reason metadata on br-5zmkr + br-619oy

Routine two-line beads metadata sync on two already-closed audit
records:

- `flywheel_connectors-5zmkr — [REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-crypto:
  FrostSigningNonces still duplicate via serde round-trip`. Status
  remains `closed`; close_reason/closed_at metadata re-synced to
  reflect the final landing commit hash from the FROST
  deserialize block (commit 9e0fe660 — `harden(fcp-crypto): block
  FrostSigningNonces deserialize to close serde nonce-duplication
  hole`).
- `flywheel_connectors-619oy — [REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-e2e/terraform:
  adapter fabricates handshake response instead of verifying
  connector contract`. Status remains `closed`; close_reason/
  closed_at metadata re-synced to reflect the final landing
  commit hash from the terraform handshake contract fix (commit
  d18041eb — `fix(fcp-e2e/terraform): drive real HandshakeRequest
  through connector + assert response contract`).

No issue bodies changed; no new beads filed; line count unchanged
(2 lines modified, 0 insertions, 0 deletions). This is the
tail-end reconciliation of the REVIEW sweep's close metadata.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5y7","title":"fcp-config: Configuration Validation and Schema Framework","description":"# fcp-config: Configuration Validation and Schema Framework

## Overview
Type-safe configuration framework with JSON Schema validation, secret handling, and environment variable support for FCP connectors.

## Schema Validation
- JSON Schema Draft 7 validation
- Required field enforcement
- Type validation (string, number, bool, array, object)
- Format validation (uri, email, uuid, datetime)
- Custom validators
- Nested schema support

## Configuration Loading
- TOML file parsing
- JSON file parsing
- Environment variable expansion (${VAR})
- File path resolution
- Default value injection
- Configuration merging

## Secret Handling
- Secret placeholder syntax: ${secret:name}
- Environment variable secrets
- File-based secrets (Docker secrets)
- Vault integration (future)
- No secrets in logs/errors

## Validation Errors
- Human-readable error messages
- JSON path to invalid field
- Expected vs actual type
- Suggested fixes
- Multiple error aggregation

## Configuration Schema Definition
- Derive macros for Rust structs
- Automatic schema generation
- Documentation extraction

## Features
- Hot reload support
- Change detection
- Validation caching
- Cross-field validation

## Dependencies
- serde, serde_json, toml
- jsonschema, regex
- fcp-core","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:49:58.772040571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:33.857911504Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:33.857911504Z","close_reason":"Superseded by FCP2 Connector Manifest (1n78.19). Configuration validation is now part of manifest parsing/verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5yw","title":"fcp.figma: Component extraction and inspection","description":"# fcp.figma: Component extraction and inspection (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose Figma component metadata so agents can:
- identify reusable components and variants
- inspect component properties (auto-layout, constraints)
- support design-to-code workflows

## Scope
- List components and component sets for a file or library.
- Fetch component details:
  - name
  - key/id
  - description
  - variant properties (for sets)
- Find component instances within a file (where supported).

## Performance constraints
- Figma files can be very large; component discovery must be:
  - paginated
  - bounded memory
  - avoid downloading full node trees unless explicitly requested

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.components.read`
- `figma.files.read` (for node references)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Treat component names/descriptions as tainted.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.component.list`
  - `figma.component.get`
  - `figma.component_set.list`
  - `figma.component.instances`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - listing pagination
  - large-file behavior (bounded)
  - error mapping (missing component, permission)

## Acceptance criteria
- Component discovery is reliable and does not require full-file downloads by default.

Operations: figma.get_file_components, figma.get_file_styles. Both return component/style metadata. 83 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:01:08Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-5zmkr","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-crypto: FrostSigningNonces still duplicate via serde round-trip","description":"crates/fcp-crypto/src/frost.rs:319-331, 1030-1048

Repro: call commit() to obtain FrostSigningNonces, serialize it with any serde backend, deserialize two copies, then call sign() twice on two different messages with the duplicated nonce package. Removing Clone in 1c52092b only blocks one duplication path; public Serialize/Deserialize still bypass the move-only single-use guarantee the API docs advertise.

Suggested fix: do not expose generic Serialize/Deserialize on FrostSigningNonces. Replace it with an explicit sealed persistence envelope or typestate-backed consume-on-load API that cannot yield two live nonce packages from one serialized blob.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:29:20.852893Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:34:29.883001Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Repro: call commit() to obtain FrostSigningNonces, serialize it with any serde backend, deserialize two copies, then call sign() twice on two different messages with the duplicated nonce package. Removing Clone in 1c52092b only blocks one duplication path; public Serialize/Deserialize still bypass the move-only single-use guarantee the API docs advertise.

Suggested fix: do not expose generic Serialize/Deserialize on FrostSigningNonces. Replace it with an explicit sealed persistence envelope or typestate-backed consume-on-load API that cannot yield two live nonce packages from one serialized blob.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:29:20.852893Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:43:07.770029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:43:07.769719Z","close_reason":"Fixed by 9e0fe660: FrostSigningNonces now rejects serde deserialization and has bincode/ciborium regression tests. Required remote cargo test still hits unrelated pre-existing cose_golden failure at crates/fcp-crypto/tests/cose_golden.rs:153.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-602cb","title":"DocuSign: replace placeholder stream_events with honest error or real Connect logs polling","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:09.522778Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:15:46.296080Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:15:46.295755Z","close_reason":"Completed in shared HEAD (commit 6f0afc9c and associated hardening follow-up). Azure slice has explicit completion note in bead comments; DocuSign now returns an honest not-implemented error for stream_connect_events; LLM-router now returns an explicit routing decision with dispatch_required/dispatch_instruction instead of implying inference was performed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","placeholders"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-602cb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ohw06","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:23.333462Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1944,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-602cb","author":"jemanuel","text":"AFFECTED FILE: connectors/docusign/src/connector.rs:655-656

CURRENT STATE:
The stream_events operation is declared in the manifest and handled in invoke(), but the implementation returns an empty events array with a comment: 'Streaming is a placeholder — real implementation would poll Connect logs or use a webhook receiver. For now, return an empty events array.'

This means an agent calling docusign.stream_events gets an empty successful response, which is misleading — it looks like there are simply no events rather than that the feature is unimplemented.

FIX OPTIONS:
A) Implement real streaming via DocuSign Connect: Poll Connect change logs for envelope status changes. DocuSign Connect sends webhook notifications for envelope events; this connector could either register a webhook or poll the Connect logs API.
B) Remove stream_events from the manifest: If streaming isn't supported, don't declare the operation.
C) Return an explicit 'not implemented' error: Change the response to FcpError::NotImplemented with a clear message.

RECOMMENDATION: Option C as immediate fix (honest error), Option A as a future enhancement if there's user demand for DocuSign event streaming.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- stream_events either works or returns a clear not-implemented error
- No empty-array 'success' response that hides the lack of implementation
- doctor() reports streaming support status honestly","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:23Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-619oy","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-e2e/terraform: adapter fabricates handshake response instead of verifying connector contract","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/terraform_compliance_e2e.rs:70-100 calls handle_handshake with stub JSON and discards the connector response, then fabricates HandshakeResponse with a fresh SessionId and echoed requested capabilities. connectors/terraform/src/connector.rs:243-262 actually returns only simple JSON fields (protocol_version/connector_id/version/capabilities). The suite therefore cannot detect handshake contract drift in nonce/session_id/capability grant behavior. Fix: serialize the real HandshakeRequest into the connector, assert on the connector-returned payload, and stop synthesizing a compliant HandshakeResponse in the adapter.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:53.083140Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:34:12.440482Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-619oy","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-e2e/terraform: adapter fabricates handshake response instead of verifying connector contract","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/terraform_compliance_e2e.rs:70-100 calls handle_handshake with stub JSON and discards the connector response, then fabricates HandshakeResponse with a fresh SessionId and echoed requested capabilities. connectors/terraform/src/connector.rs:243-262 actually returns only simple JSON fields (protocol_version/connector_id/version/capabilities). The suite therefore cannot detect handshake contract drift in nonce/session_id/capability grant behavior. Fix: serialize the real HandshakeRequest into the connector, assert on the connector-returned payload, and stop synthesizing a compliant HandshakeResponse in the adapter.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:53.083140Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:43:03.026756Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:43:03.026204Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2843,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-619oy","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fix landed in commit d18041eb (concurrent sibling commit; my equivalent edit was rebased away before I could commit, but the identical fix shipped — adapter now serializes the real HandshakeRequest, drives it through connector.handle_handshake, and asserts protocol_version/connector_id/capability intersection on the returned payload). Verified via rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-e2e --test terraform_compliance_e2e → 3/3 green.","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:43:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","title":"[MOR/C3.4] Make capability constraints mandatory; reject null/empty constraints","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:57.050666Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:50:57.007185Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T23:50:57.006485Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0-critical","capability","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-800e3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:41.067762Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8whb3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:06.752983Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ky3yv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:09.737664Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2643,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Make capability constraints mandatory in CapabilityToken. Reject tokens with null/empty/missing constraints. Define a DENY-ALL default when constraints are absent (empty resource list = no operations allowed). WHY: H2 Adversarial found capability ceiling constraint bypass (#9, HIGH): if constraints are None, enforce_resource_constraints() is never called. An attacker-controlled issuer can issue unconstrained capabilities to arbitrary resources. J1 Deontic found this violates the default-deny principle — a null constraint should mean no access, not unlimited access. WHERE: crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs — CapabilityToken verification path, specifically the constraint enforcement section. Change: if constraints is None, return Err(FcpError::CapabilityDenied) instead of skipping. Also update CapabilityToken construction to require constraints. ACCEPTANCE: (1) Tokens without constraints are rejected at verification time. (2) Token construction API requires constraints parameter (not Optional). (3) Empty constraint set = deny all (not allow all). (4) At least 4 tests: token with valid constraints passes, token with null constraints rejected, token with empty constraints rejected, token with overly-broad constraints still works (constraint narrowing is separate). ESTIMATED EFFORT: 1-2 days. DESIGN NOTE: This is one of the highest-impact-to-effort-ratio changes. Removing one Option<> wrapper eliminates an entire class of privilege escalation. DEPENDS ON: C3.1 (phantom tokens) — because the constraint check happens during verify(), and the phantom type ensures verify() is always called.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:36:49Z"},{"id":2648,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","author":"jemanuel","text":"CROSS-EPIC DEP: Depends on C1.3 (RevocationFreshnessClass in manifests) because both C1.3 and C3.4 add mandatory fields to the capability/manifest validation path. They should be coordinated to avoid two separate breaking changes to the validation pipeline. Implement them together in the same verification method.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:41Z"},{"id":2663,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-62ai5","author":"jemanuel","text":"C3.4 mandatory constraints: CapabilityConstraints::is_empty(), verification rejects null/empty, constraints_cbor() on CwtClaims+Builder, 4 acceptance tests. Commit 45ecad49.","created_at":"2026-04-10T23:50:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-63o","title":"fcp.zendesk: Ticket CRUD Operations","description":"# fcp.zendesk: Ticket CRUD Operations (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe, typed ticket CRUD so agents can:
- create tickets
- read ticket details and thread metadata
- update status/priority/assignee/tags

Ticket content often contains PII; strict logging hygiene is required.

## Scope
- Create/get/update ticket.
- List/search tickets with filters and pagination.
- Soft-delete/archive (dangerous; optional).
- Attachments are handled as bounded downloads/uploads (may be separate surface).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `zendesk.tickets.read`
- `zendesk.tickets.write` (dangerous-ish)
- Optional: `zendesk.tickets.delete` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to the Zendesk subdomain host.
- PII hygiene:
  - do not log raw ticket bodies or requester emails
  - logs include only ticket ids and field names changed
- For writes: receipts + audit events required.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `zendesk.ticket.create`
  - `zendesk.ticket.get`
  - `zendesk.ticket.update`
  - `zendesk.ticket.search`
  - Optional: `zendesk.ticket.archive`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - CRUD flows
  - pagination
  - permission denied
  - receipts/audit for mutations
  - redaction

## Acceptance criteria
- Ticket mutations are mechanically authorized and auditable.
- No PII leaks into logs.

Operations: zendesk.create_ticket, zendesk.get_ticket, zendesk.update_ticket, zendesk.delete_ticket, zendesk.search_tickets, zendesk.list_ticket_comments. 58 tests pass (34 unit + 24 integration). Includes capability gating, introspection metadata, and error handling.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:00:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-691o","title":"[IDEA-11.3] Streaming health tests + simulated loop","description":"## Goal
Provide tests for streaming health behavior using a simulated loop.

## Details
- Simulate missed heartbeat and ensure state transitions.
- Verify log fields emitted per schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests validate deterministic health transitions.","notes":"Implemented streaming heartbeat timeout unit test in fcp-sdk runtime + existing supervisor heartbeat timeout/log test covers missed heartbeat + log fields. Gates: fmt/check/clippy green. Waiting on parent flywheel_connectors-j7w1 to close.

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.
